zh.Clifton, Lancaster, England: 英國英格蘭藍卡爾克里夫頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Clifton於1202年出生在英國藍卡爾克里夫頓時，他的父親Walter de Clifton42歲，母親Egelina de Clifton30歲。他和Lady Ellen Warren至少有3個兒子。他於1249年被列為knighted。他去世於1258年3月29日之前在他的家鄉。

zh-hans.Clifton, Lancaster, England: 英国英格兰藍卡爾克里夫頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Clifton于1202年出生在英国藍卡爾克里夫頓时，他的父亲Walter de Clifton42岁，母亲Egelina de Clifton30岁。他和Lady Ellen Warren至少有3个儿子。他于1249年被列为knighted。他去世于1258年3月29日之前在他的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Clifton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of the very numerous places called Clifton, from Old English clif ‘slope’ (see Cliff ) + tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Cliff
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called Cliff(e), Cle(e)ve, or Clive, from Old English clif ‘slope, bank, cliff’, or a topographic name from the same word. The Old English word was used not only in the sense of modern English cliff but also of much gentler slopes and frequently also of a riverbank or seashore.

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Clifton was born in 1202, in Clifton, Lancashire, England, his father, Walter de Clifton, was 42 and his mother, Egelina de Clifton, was 30. He had at least 3 sons with Lady Ellen Warren. In 1249, at the age of 49, his occupation is listed as knighted. He died before 29 March 1258, in his hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Clifton est né en 1202, à Clifton, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Walter de Clifton, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Egelina de Clifton, avait 30 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Lady Ellen Warren. En 1249, à l’âge de 49 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est knighted. Il est décédé avant 29 mars 1258, dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Clifton 1202 in Clifton, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Walter de Clifton 42 und seine Mutter Egelina de Clifton 30. Er hatte mit Lady Ellen Warren mindestens 3 Söhne. 1249, als er 49 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als knighted aufgeführt. Er starb vor 29. März 1258 in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de Clifton è nato il nel 1202 nel Clifton, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Walter de Clifton, aveva 42 anni e il suo madre, Egelina de Clifton, aveva 30 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Lady Ellen Warren. Nel 1249, all'età di 49 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come knighted. Morì prima del 29 marzo 1258, nel sua cittadina.

ja.Clifton, Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイアクリフトン

ja.Lancashire, England: イギリスランカシュアイア
ja.lifeSummary: William de Cliftonが1202年にイギリスランカシュアイアクリフトンで生まれた時，父Walter de Cliftonは42歳，母Egelina de Cliftonは30歳であった。彼はLady Ellen Warrenとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。1249年，49歳の時，職業はknightedと記録されている。彼は1258年3月29日以前に故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Clifton(이)가 1202년 영국 랭카실르 클리프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Walter de Clifton(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Egelina de Clifton(은)는 30세였다. 그는 Lady Ellen Warren(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 1249년, 49세의 나이로 그의 직업은 knighted이었다. 그는 1258년 3월 29일 이전 그의 고향에서 사망했다.

mn.Clifton, Lancaster, England: Clifton, Lancashire, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de Clifton nasceu em 1202, em Clifton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Walter de Clifton, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Egelina de Clifton, tinha 30 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Lady Ellen Warren. Em 1249, com 49 anos, sua profissão aparece como knighted. Ele faleceu antes 29 de março de 1258, em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Clifton родился в 1202 г. в местности клифтон, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Walter de Clifton было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Egelina de Clifton, 30. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Lady Ellen Warren. В 1249 году, в возрасте 49 лет,  он работал по профессии knighted. Он умер перед 29 марта 1258 г. в его родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de Clifton nació en 1202, en Clifton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, Walter de Clifton, tenía 42 años y su mamá, Egelina de Clifton, tenía 30 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Lady Ellen Warren. En 1249, a la edad de 49 años, su ocupación registrada era knighted. Murió antes de 29 de marzo de 1258, en su pueblo.
